Title: Inhibition of DCL4 activity by maternally supplied flavonoid aglycons induces a bicolor pattern in the saddle soybean seed coat

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: flavonoid biosynthesis; phenylpropanoid pathway; anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry/committed step to general flavonoid and anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for seed coat flavonoid/anthocyanin production in this PTGS system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat pigmentation affecting soybean product processing and human health-promoting properties of anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed coat, especially central region around the hilum in saddle cultivar and black seed coat in black cultivar
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in pigmented seed coat; expression is suppressed by PTGS in yellow cultivar and in peripheral region of saddle seed coat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When CHS PTGS is suppressed in the central region, flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis resumes and pigmentation develops; when PTGS is active, seed coat remains colorless.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CHS-derived siRNA detection by Northern hybridization; region-specific PTGS analysis; metabolite profiling; inference from DCL4 inhibition model
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: immature seed developmental stage (DS1-DS3); in vitro culture of detached immature seeds reduces phenolic compounds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter; PTGS context under endogenous I or i^j locus
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS is the PTGS target whose silencing determines whether soybean seed coat pigmentation occurs. 2. In saddle soybean, PTGS against CHS is suppressed specifically in the central region around the hilum, allowing local flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis. 3. Inhibition of DCL4 by maternally supplied flavonoid aglycons, especially quercetin, is proposed to block CHS silencing and generate the bicolor saddle pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CHS is central to the mechanism but not the main experimentally manipulated focal gene; black cultivar Tan lacks the inverted repeat CHS trigger required for PTGS.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating CHS silencing domains or combining CHS/PTGS alleles with regulators of flavonoid transport may enable engineering of seed coat pigmentation patterns and anthocyanin content.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased anthocyanin pigmentation may negatively affect processing quality of soybean products, though it may improve nutraceutical value.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: RNA silencing enzyme; Dicer-like RNase III endoribonuclease
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional gene silencing via dsRNA dicing; activity is inhibited by flavonoid aglycons including quercetin
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: post-transcriptional silencing of CHS, thereby controlling flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes production of 21-nt siRNAs that silence CHS and suppress flavonoid biosynthesis; inhibition of DCL4 relieves CHS silencing.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential local switch determining whether seed coat flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis is repressed or allowed; region-specific inhibition explains saddle pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: maternally supplied flavonoid aglycons via funiculus; especially quercetin; developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward loop proposed: imported and de novo synthesized flavonoid aglycons inhibit DCL4, which relieves CHS silencing and promotes further flavonoid production.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat pigmentation and nutraceutical anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed coat central region around hilum in saddle soybean; black seed coat regions in black cultivar
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: DCL4 transcript levels showed no significant regional difference between central and peripheral seed coat or among developmental stages, despite strong regional differences in activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active DCL4 maintains CHS PTGS and colorless seed coat; inhibition of DCL4 in the central region permits local flavonoid/anthocyanin accumulation and bicolor pattern formation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro dicing assay; regional crude extract comparison; extract mixing inhibition assay; PVPP depletion rescue; addition of commercial quercetin; CHS siRNA Northern blot; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed coat region (central vs peripheral); immature seed developmental stage DS1-DS3; PVPP treatment; quercetin addition; detached seed in vitro culture
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS silencing machinery components not directly tested; functional interaction with flavonoid aglycons including quercetin
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCL4 dicing activity was detected in yellow seed coat and the peripheral region of saddle seed coat, but not in the central pigmented region of saddle seed coat or black cultivar seed coat. 2. DCL4 transcript abundance did not explain the regional difference, indicating post-transcriptional enzymatic inhibition. 3. Crude extracts from the pigmented central region inhibited dicing activity, PVPP treatment restored activity, and quercetin directly inhibited DCL4 in vitro. 4. The study proposes that maternally supplied flavonoid aglycons locally inhibit DCL4 around the hilum, thereby suppressing PTGS and inducing the saddle pattern.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although DCL3 was also inhibited by quercetin and in pigmented tissues, the paper identifies DCL4 as the critical PTGS factor linked to CHS siRNA production and saddle pattern formation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeted modulation of DCL4 activity or its sensitivity to flavonoid aglycons could be used to tune seed coat pigmentation pattern and flavonoid accumulation, but may broadly perturb RNA silencing pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing DCL4 activity may affect broader RNA silencing, potentially causing off-target developmental or epigenetic effects beyond seed coat pigmentation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: RNA silencing enzyme; Dicer-like RNase III endoribonuclease
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: dicing of dsRNA into 24-nt small RNAs; activity inhibited by flavonoids including quercetin in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: associated silencing pathways linked indirectly to flavonoid-rich pigmented tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Not directly mapped to CHS silencing in this paper; dicing activity decreases in pigmented tissues and recovers after PVPP treatment.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not the main determinant, but co-inhibited with DCL4 in pigmented seed coat regions.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: flavonoid aglycons including quercetin; developmental stage; seed coat region
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript level did not significantly differ between regions/stages in tested seed coats.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DCL3 activity is absent in flavonoid-rich pigmented regions and can be restored by PVPP treatment, indicating inhibition by accumulated phenolics/flavonoids.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro dicing assay; extract mixing assay; PVPP depletion rescue; quercetin addition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed developmental stage; seed coat region; PVPP treatment; quercetin addition
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: functional interaction with flavonoid aglycons including quercetin
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCL3 dicing activity was detected in yellow seed coat and peripheral saddle seed coat but not in the flavonoid-rich central region of saddle seed coat. 2. Pigmented-region extracts inhibited DCL3 activity, and PVPP treatment restored it. 3. Quercetin inhibited DCL3 in vitro, suggesting broader suppression of RNA silencing in flavonoid-rich tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core co-affected regulator because it was directly assayed in the Results, but the main causal connection to CHS PTGS and saddle pigmentation is stronger for DCL4.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely not an ideal direct breeding target because of broader silencing roles and less specific evidence for seed coat color control than DCL4.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may affect transcriptional gene silencing and genome regulation more broadly.
